events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need classify condition and determine whether defect requiring corrective construction work. Scenario: one-year warranty walkthrough, non-fire-rated interior partition, three small circular bumps in paint film along one stud line, consistent with seasonal lumber shrinkage. Fastener spacing met schedule, no cracking, movement, moisture staining. Reference GA-216 (Gypsum Association Application and Finishing of Gypsum Panel Products). Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. This is accepted stage. Screw pops due to lumber shrinkage are common and often considered cosmetic, not a defect requiring corrective construction if no structural/performance issue. Need assess per GA-216: screw pops can be caused by lumber shrinkage, not necessarily installation defect. Since spacing met, no cracking/moisture, likely not non-compliant. Decision: pass? But question asks classify condition and determine whether it represents a defect requiring corrective construction work. It may be a cosmetic defect but not requiring corrective construction. In benchmark, decision likely pass because no code/manufacturer violation. Component conditions none. Risk low. Work_stage accepted. percent_complete 100? accepted one-year walkthrough. value 0. confidence high. Findings: minor screw pops from seasonal shrinkage; fastener spacing compliant; no cracking/movement/moisture. Actions: cosmetic touch-up if desired, monitor; no corrective construction required. Rationale: GA-216 recognizes shrinkage-induced fastener pops as a materials/movement phenomenon, not an installation defect, when spacing and finishing are correct and no performance issues. References: GA-216. Need ensure JSON valid.
